label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 13

extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won stage 13 after Ackermann went extremely long on the final climb, Pedersen drafting him then coming around late.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo deliberately raised tempo mid-stage to fracture sprinter trains despite not having pure speed advantage.
Ackermann's ultra-long leadout from 500m+ on 7% climb created draft opportunity Pedersen exploited rather than attacking early.
Headwind inside 10km caused tactical paralysis with compact peloton, negating earlier aggressive positioning by multiple teams.

rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: won without dedicated leadout on climbing finish, showing strength versatility Trek may exploit on semi-hard stages
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: went far too early on gradient finish, tactical error suggests UAE lacks experience closing on climbing sprints
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: positive: fourth place without visible team support on climbing finish reveals underrated punch for hilly sprint stages
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: commentary notes pressure coming on mountain stages this weekend with comfortable GC margin currently
